column12: ID=g15710.t1;Parent=g15710;em_target=48698.ENSPFOP00000009451;em_score=713.0;em_evalue=5.86e-263;em_tcov=100.0;em_OGs=COG5235@1|root,KOG3108@2759|Eukaryota,38IVJ@33154|Opisthokonta,3BHFU@33208|Metazoa,3CSBV@33213|Bilateria,489D4@7711|Chordata,490AF@7742|Vertebrata,49Y93@7898|Actinopterygii;em_COG_cat=B;em_desc=Component of the CST complex proposed to act as a specialized replication factor promoting DNA replication under conditions of replication stress or natural replication barriers such as the telomere duplex. The CST complex binds single-stranded DNA with high affinity in a sequence-independent manner, while isolated subunits bind DNA with low affinity by themselves. Initially the CST complex has been proposed to protect telomeres from DNA degradation.
